title: Madagascar declares state of emergency over severe fuel shortages linked to Iran war
content: The presidency says it is taking action as it fears fuel shortages could lead to public disorder.

originalTitle: Cameroon 'military contractors' killed in Russia-Ukraine war - BBC confirms leaked memo
title: Sad News from Far Away: Our Friends from Cameroon
content:
Jambo, wonderful children of Africa! It’s your friendly news reporter here, ready to share some news with you. Today, we have some news from far, far away, about some grown-ups from our very own continent, Africa.

What Happened Far Away?

There's a beautiful country called Cameroon. It's in Africa, just like us! It has many friendly people and different kinds of places, like big mountains, sunny beaches, and green forests. Far, far away from Cameroon, there are two other countries. One is called Ukraine, and the other is Russia. These countries are a very long way from Africa. Sadly, these two countries are having a very big fight, called a war. A war is when countries fight with soldiers and big noisy machines. It makes people sad and hurts places. Some grown-ups from Cameroon went to Ukraine. They went there to work. They were called 'contractors'. Imagine someone who gets hired to do a special job, maybe helping out. But this job was very dangerous because it was in a place where there was a war. Sadly, we found out that some of these grown-ups from Cameroon died in that faraway fight. This makes us feel very sad for their families and friends.

Who Are These 'Contractors'?

When we say 'contractors' in this story, it means people who are not official soldiers for Ukraine or Russia. They are like helpers or workers who get paid to do jobs. Sometimes, grown-ups go to other countries to find work and earn money. They want to help their families have food and a good home. But it's important to remember that war is very, very dangerous. People can get hurt, and places can be broken.

What Did Cameroon Say?

Our governments usually like to tell us when their people are safe at home. They want to make sure everyone is okay. But for a long time, the government of Cameroon didn't talk much about its people who were working in the war in Ukraine. It's like when you have a secret, and you don't talk about it very much. They didn't want to say if their citizens were fighting or helping in the war. But a big news group, like a team of reporters, found out the sad news. They saw a special paper, like a secret message called a 'memo', that said these grown-ups from Cameroon had died. It’s important for news reporters to share true stories with everyone, even if they are sad. They help us understand what is happening in the world.

Did You Know?

Did you know that Cameroon is sometimes called 'Africa in miniature'? That's because it has so many different kinds of places, like mountains, beaches, and forests, just like our whole continent! Did you know that peace is when everyone lives together without fighting? When there is peace, children can go to school, play outside, and families can be happy and safe. Did you know that many people in Africa speak different languages? In Cameroon, people speak English and French, plus many local languages! How cool is that? Did you know that news reporters work very hard to find out what is happening in the world? They talk to people, read important papers, and travel to many places to get the real story. Did you know that children all over the world wish for peace? We all want a world where everyone is kind to each other and no one has to fight. Did you know that staying safe is the most important thing? Grown-ups always try to keep children safe, and we should always listen to them when they tell us to be careful. Did you know that the capital city of Cameroon is called Yaoundé? It’s a busy city where many people live and work! Did you know that many countries in Africa help each other? We are like one big family, and we care about what happens to our neighbours and friends. Did you know that even though wars happen, many people work very hard to bring peace? They talk to each other and try to find ways to stop fighting and be friends again.

Why Is This Important?

This news reminds us that war is a very serious thing. It takes people away from their homes and families forever. War makes many people sad. It also reminds us that people from Africa travel all over the world. Sometimes they go for good reasons, like studying or working in safe jobs to help their families. But sometimes, they might go to places that are not safe. It's good to know what is happening, even if it makes us a little sad. Knowing helps us understand the world better. We should always wish for peace for everyone, everywhere. We want all children and grown-ups to be safe.

Let's Wish for Peace!

It's so important that we all learn to be kind to each other. At home, at school, and in our communities, we can show love and kindness every day. When we are kind, we help make our world a more peaceful place for everyone. Let’s make our little corner of the world full of peace. Let's hope that one day, all big fights like wars will stop, and everyone can live safely with their families. Let’s send a big wish for peace and safety to everyone in Cameroon and all around the world. Thank you for listening, children. Stay safe and spread kindness!
summary: Some people from Cameroon, an African country, went to work far away in a country called Ukraine, where there is a war, and sadly, some of them died.
whyMatters: It's important to know that war is very dangerous and takes people away from their families, and we should always wish for peace and safety for everyone in the world.

title: Plan to scrap presidential elections puts Zimbabweans at loggerheads
content: The ruling party has unveiled a draft law that strips voters of the chance to elect the president.

title: World Athletics postpones Doha Diamond League amid US-Israel war on Iran
content: The multi-discipline event scheduled for May 8 will now be held on June 19 should conditions allow, organisers say.

originalTitle: Mumbai’s historic dock quietens as fuel crisis chokes fishing trade
title: Sad Boats in a Big City: Why Fishers Can't Catch Fish!
content:
Hello, wonderful friends across Africa! I'm your friendly news reporter, and I have a story for you from a faraway place called Mumbai. Mumbai is a big, busy city in a country called India, right next to the big blue sea. Guess what? Something is making many, many boats very sad there.

Sleepy Boats at Sassoon Dock

Imagine a big parking lot for boats. That's what a 'dock' is! The place we are talking about is called Sassoon Dock. It is a very important dock for fishermen in Mumbai. Usually, Sassoon Dock is full of noise and happy people. Fishermen get their boats ready very early in the morning. Big baskets full of fresh fish come off the boats. It's a very busy place, with lots of people buying and selling fish. But now, many fishing boats are just sitting there. They look sleepy and quiet. They are not going out to catch fish. Why are they just sitting still? Let's find out together.

What is Fuel and Why is it Important?

Our cars and buses need a special liquid to move, right? This liquid is called 'fuel.' Fuel helps engines make power so things can go. For big boats that go far into the ocean, this special fuel is called 'diesel.' Diesel makes the big engines in the boats go 'vroom vroom!' Without diesel, the boats cannot sail far into the ocean. They cannot go to where the fish live. It's like a bicycle without pedals – it just can't go anywhere! Did you know? Diesel helps big trucks move food and other important things all over the world too! It's a very important kind of fuel for many big machines.

Fuel Too Expensive!

Now, here is the big problem in Mumbai: diesel has become very, very expensive. It costs too much money. Imagine your favorite candy suddenly costing ten times more! You might not be able to buy it anymore, right? It's the same for the fishermen. They need a lot, a lot of diesel for just one trip to catch fish. If the diesel costs too much, they spend all their money just to get to the fishing spots. Then, even if they catch fish, they don't have enough money left to feed their families. So, many fishermen have to keep their boats at the dock. They simply cannot afford to buy the diesel they need to go fishing. It makes them feel very sad.

No Fish, No Money for Families

When the boats don't go out, what happens? That's right! No fish are caught! And if there are no fish, the fishermen cannot sell them at the market. This means the fishermen don't earn any money. This is called 'livelihood.' A livelihood is how people earn money to live, to buy food, clothes, and send their children to school. Think about your parents' jobs. They work hard and earn money so your family can have what it needs. When fishermen can't work, their families have a very hard time. It means less food, less money for school books, and no new clothes. It affects many, many people. Families worry a lot. Did you know? Fish is a very healthy food! It has special things that help your brain grow big and strong. Many people love to eat fish because it's so yummy and good for you!

How This Affects Everyone

It's not just the fishermen and their families who are sad. If there's no fish from the boats, then the fish shops don't have fish to sell. People who love to eat fish might not find any in the market. Or the fish they find might be even more expensive. This also makes the 'economy' sad. The economy is like a big game where everyone works and trades things. When one part, like fishing, stops working, it can make other parts slow down too. Imagine a chain. If one link breaks, the whole chain can't work properly. Fishing is a very important link in the economy for places near the sea, like Mumbai. Many jobs depend on fishing, not just the fishermen. Did you know? Some fish travel very long distances in the ocean! They are amazing swimmers and can go thousands of kilometres. They need big, strong boats to catch them!

A Wish for Hope

The fishermen and their families in Mumbai are wishing for a solution. They hope that the price of diesel will become cheaper soon. This would make their boats happy again! They want to go back to sea, catch fish, and bring smiles to people's faces with fresh, yummy fish. They want to earn money so their children can go to school and have new clothes and good food. Just like in some of our fishing villages here in Africa, people rely on the sea for their food and jobs. When things get tough, we all hope for better days and helping hands. Let's wish for brighter days for the fishermen of Mumbai, and for all people everywhere who work hard to feed their families! We hope the sleepy boats can soon sail out to sea again!
summary: Many fishing boats in a city called Mumbai can't go to sea because the special fuel they need is too expensive, making it hard for fishermen to earn money and catch fish for everyone.
whyMatters: This is important because when fishermen can't work, families don't have money for food and school, and people don't get healthy fish to eat.

originalTitle: US ‘third-country’ deportees deal sparks backlash in DRC
title: Big News from Congo: People Are Talking About a Special Deal!
content:
Hello from Africa!

Hi everyone! It's me, your friendly news reporter, here to tell you exciting and important stories from our amazing continent, Africa! Today, we're going on a special learning adventure to a very big and beautiful country called the Democratic Republic of Congo, or DRC for short. It's a place full of tall trees, amazing animals, and wonderful people.

What's the Big News Today?

Imagine two big friends, like two countries, making a plan together. Well, something like that is happening! The country called the United States of America (that's a country far, far away across the big ocean) and our friend, the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC), have made a special agreement. This agreement is about sending some people to live in the DRC. Now, here's the tricky part: these people are *not* originally from the DRC. They are from *other* different countries all over the world. But the United States wants to send them to the DRC. This is what we call a 'deal' or an 'agreement' between two governments.

Who Are These People Being Sent?

Sometimes, people travel to a new country to find a safer home or a new job. But if they don't have special papers or permission to stay in that country, the government might ask them to leave. When a country asks someone to leave, it's called 'deporting' them. The people who are asked to leave are called 'deportees'. So, the news is that the United States wants to send people who they asked to leave *their* country to the DRC. Remember, these people are not from the DRC! They are from other places, maybe even faraway lands that aren't in Africa at all. It's like your friend inviting someone else's friend to stay at *your* house, even though you don't know them!

Why Do Countries Send People Away?

Countries have rules, just like your school or your home has rules. One rule is about who can live there and for how long. People usually need special papers, like a visa or a passport stamp, to stay in a country. If someone stays without these papers, or if their papers expire, a country might ask them to go. Sometimes, people have to leave their own country because it's not safe there, maybe because of a war or trouble. They might try to find safety in another country. It's a very big and sometimes sad problem for many people around the world.

Why Are Some People in DRC Worried?

Now, even though the DRC government made this deal, some people *in* the DRC are not happy about it. They are worried! When many people are worried and say 'no' to something their government does, we call it a 'backlash'. It means they are pushing back with their voices because they have concerns. Why are they worried? Well, the DRC is a very big country, but it already has many people who need homes, food, and schools. Many people who live in the DRC are already helping other people who have had to leave their homes from *inside* Africa. It's a lot of work to make sure everyone is safe and has what they need. So, when the news came that even *more* people, who are not even from Africa, might be coming to live in the DRC, some people felt it wasn't fair. They worry that the DRC might not have enough space, enough food, or enough schools for everyone, especially for people who have no family or friends there. They want to make sure that the people already in the DRC, and the people from other African countries they are already helping, are looked after first. It's like when you have a special treat to share, you want to make sure your family and close friends get some before you share it with people you don't know very well, especially if you only have a little bit left!

Let's Learn About the Democratic Republic of Congo!

The DRC is a truly amazing place! It's the second-largest country in all of Africa. That's super big! It has lots of different parts, from huge green rainforests to big rivers and tall mountains. **Did you know?** The DRC is home to some incredible animals that you can only find there, like the okapi (which looks a bit like a zebra and a giraffe mixed together!) and magnificent mountain gorillas! It's a land of incredible nature. The capital city of the DRC is called Kinshasa. It's a very lively city with lots of music, dancing, and busy markets! People in the DRC speak many different languages, but French is one of the main ones. The people of the DRC are known for their strong communities and rich cultures. They work hard and love their country very much. They are always trying to make their country a better place for everyone.

What Happens Next?

When people have worries like this, it's important for everyone to talk. The government of the DRC is listening to what its people are saying. And governments from different countries, like the USA and the DRC, will continue to talk to each other to find the best way forward. We hope that they can find a solution that is fair and kind to everyone involved – to the people who need a new home, and to the wonderful people of the Democratic Republic of Congo.

Being Kind to Everyone

This story reminds us that even though countries have borders, we are all part of one big world family. It's always important to be kind, to understand each other, and to help those who need it. But it's also important to make sure that everyone has what they need in their own homes and communities first. Thank you for learning with me today! See you next time for more news from Africa!
summary: The United States wants to send people who are not from the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) to live there, but many people in the DRC are worried and unhappy about this special agreement.
whyMatters: This is important because it shows how countries make big decisions, and how sometimes people in a country might worry if their home can help even more new people, especially when they are already helping many others.
